channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 101 replies — 111.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-06-03 22:29 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

62296711
BEHOLD, THE FUTURE

62296738
>>62296711

62297476
AAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62298184
>>62296711
Is that you Michael?

62298251
>>62284814
>buy signal
>5/28
This aged like milk.

62299491
>>62298251
>can't read
Maybe investing is not for you anon

>>62298184
Shhh it's a secret

62301262
>>62292563
>>62292730
what's a hyperliquid vault

62301440
>tfw if I listened to the schizo chart I would be down 10%

62301542
All i can see is that you've been incorrect? Am i missing something? Lol

62302104
>>62301542
>>62301440
We'll see - the hit rate's 78%, not 100%. The general sense, per the twitter thread yesterday, is bounce to the 5th-7th, then drop to the real bottom mid-June and then pump through summer, but obviously Brownian motion can't predict Saylor selling lol

62303698
Tell me no one bought because of this indicator please HAHAHAHAHA

62303805
Is this some intraday trading indicator? Like you were supposed to buy and then sell with leverage the day of the OP? If you would have bought and were holding now you would be fucked

62304332
reverse indicator of op is printing me money

62305278
Look how they massacred my coin

62305335
>>62296711
I appreciate the insight. This thread has been an interesting read.

62305363
>>62305335
Thanks Anon, wish it was going better right now though!
Liquidity's good, the sim says "increasing demand," sure enough stocks are up and copper/gold is acting like a boom is coming, but crypto is just getting slaughtered. The only explanation I can think of is we're getting fucked now that CME crypto futures are legal. But without any actual data that's probably just cope

62305385
it's over

62306031
>>62305363
I had an asset do 200% in like 7 days recently, so it seems at least that people aren't turning up their noses at it all, so long as substantial developments are being made. Still not immune to dipping after considering profit-taking. Good projects can stand out in a storm like a lighthouse does. We'll see if that continues into the year.

62306711
>>62306031
Oh, I don't think crypto's over at all, unless someone has cracked SHA-256. Something is definitely happening to the asset class, though. A pro-crypto Fed chair just took over, stocks are up, but our internet coins are acting like they're about to be banned (for the tenth time)

62308784
Bump

62308863
>>62285441
You can trade at mexc and make maker orders close to the current price, that way only the taker pays 0.4-5% and you lose nothing.

62309487
>>62306031
Consistency is key.

62310796
>>62308784
I think we're fully in a news-driven cycle now, or maybe more accurately a institutional-crime-driven cycle. Physics-liquidity has no edge rn.
I'm working on this seasonality model which has caught some pivots so far, and if it's right we trend down to mid-June and then pump hard through the summer, but it's extremely experimental and I'll need months more results to compare to predictions before I could even start tuning it

62310807
>>62310796
it could also be the observer effect has broken your model

62310837
>>62310807
>the observer effect has broken your model
If Saylor's found my model he could at least send me a couple shekels
That said, about 95 times out of 100 a physics-predicted pivot that happens on a weekend gets frontrun the last trading day before, which does make me suspect institutions also figured this out

62310897
>>62285204
>the thing has a ~80% hit rate over more than a year

80% of the time it works all of the time

62310926
based

62311911
Bro..?

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-03 23:19 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62309559
It is so annoying that an entire asset class need to be defended by the financially illiterate, bear trolls and the mentally ill. Eveything is a suckers rally or a dead cat bounce.

You people talk so much about buying the dip. Then the dip happens, and you mock those who buy it.

Bearposting needs to be banned from the board

62309584
>>62309559
It seems Bitcoin is an asset, the purely image based crypto coins are the asset class.
And it seems it dipped to 60k a few weeks ago. These little spikes tend to be retested.

62310185
I had a dream where bitcoin went to $11k and then I woke up the next day and it had gone to $2k. I didn't consider buying it at those prices, I just felt happy that loads of people will have lost all their savings

62310319
>>62309535
omg

62311471
>>62309515
64k just in. Where can I find the next waiting room, good Sir?

62311487
>>62309503
thoughts?

62311869
65k got melted through like butter

62311872
>>62309503
op BTFO

62311890
bottom, now.

62311921
I’m gonna laugh so hard when Gaylor buys 200 btc tomorrow

62312109
>>62311487
>>62311869
>>62311890
>>62311487
>HE SOLD THE BOTTOM
HE SOLD THE BOTTOM
>HE SOLD THE BOTTOM
HE SOLD THE BOTTOM

At this point, if you sell BTC, I thank you for doing so because no asset class survives while held by the financially illiterate. Just look at PMs for a very grim example.

I insist on my position, and you can see the decline in price is already slowing down. Tomorrow, it'll normalize around 66k, then it'll be near 80k by the end of the month.

It is always like this. You spend ten fucking years talking about buying the bottom, then when the actual bottom is hit, you don't take the risk.

Just buy bonds and index at this point, seriously, if you still fumble the clearest bottom in history.

62312124
>>62311921
>I’m gonna laugh so hard when Gaylor buys 200 btc tomorrow

That nigga is leveraged to the max, he already had to sell to pay debts, spending 12 million after paying a 180k debt isn't likely

62312131
>>62309503
>bottom is 65k
kek no its around 45k-50k

62312142
>>62311471
Don’t move, this thread is the 62k waiting room now

62312143
>>62312142
>mfw I wake up and it's 59k

item.title: you silly basterds think btc is down because of saylor man wait until normcattle hear about quantum fud on steroids >Goo… — 13 replies — 29.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
you silly basterds think btc is down because of saylor man wait until normcattle hear about quantum fud on steroids >Goo…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-03 22:57 UTC

OP

you silly basterds think btc is down because of saylor man
wait until normcattle hear about quantum fud on steroids

>Google cracked Shor's algorithm for secp256k1 (the curve securing btc keys)
>hid the details behind a ZK proof at US government request
>a french reverse-engineered it in two months
>cryptographic qday by 2032

[Submitted on 1 Jun 2026]
Optimized Point Addition Circuits for Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithms
André Schrottenloher
https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02235

start sweating bitches

Early replies (first 3)

62306885
>>62306854
bip360. problem solved

62306909
also, he reverse-engineered it 10% better
and vibeGODS are VIBING it even better now
it's the ultimate humiliation

62307046
>>62306854
they said in that same paper that Mochimo already solved it, but also brought up QRL which is a scamcoin that Coinmarketcap CEO called out and had de-tracked due to inflated valuation. Both are supposedly quantum proof. I bought some MCM just in case. Qday is real.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62307066
>>62306854
>Saylor knew it
Is that why he sold only 0.0038% of this stash?

62308190
>>62306909
>vibeGODS
peasants who don't own AI
in a few years it will be:
legacy biomass that isn't AI

62308352
>>62306854
I'll explain cause somehow even among cryptofags there aren't any compsci people here...
"Cracking" the algorithm here actually means just halving the power of operations required to solve it. If it's 256 bit, then it's 2 to the power of 256. In the very best case scenario here, that goes to 2 to the power of 128 on a quantum processor. You will need many million times all of the current quantum processing power in the world right now to crack a single one in years, and these are extremely difficult to manufacture.

The real chokepoint for quantum computing for like 15 years now is that they just cannot make them large enough, not by a long shot. If they somehow had a breakthrough and were able to make a quantum processor 10 times as large (in terms of qubits) as the current world record, we'd still need to have like 3 more breakthroughs like that to even approach the possibility of craching a single 256 bit key.

62308570
>>62308352
The feds will make it happen by 2040. They can't stand us little piggies being able to hide things from them. Soon to fly domestically they will require a DNA sample which will be used to decide your fate during the great cull. Buy PLTR to influence your odds.

62308578
>quanum fud
literally sci fi teir fantasy nonsense lmao

62310779
>>62306854
>quantum breakaway is here !1!1!1!
>btc is fucked reee
>stocks make ath
illiterate niggers cant understand that when quantum computing is finally a reality everything goes to shit not only crapto

62310828
>>62306854
Release ze quantum fud

62310842
>Can crack any encryption on the planet
>LMAO BITCOINS IN TROUBLE
it's too easy to see through these retard posts

62312031
>>62310779
have you watched traveling salesman (the film)?

62312044
So buy a crap ton of quantum resistant stocks/coins?

item.title: SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-03 23:09 UTC

OP

GIGA DOWNTREND CONFIRMED
GET OUT BEFORE IT IS TO LATE

EVERYONE IS GETTING OUT OF CRYPTO AND PUTTING IT INTO SPACE/TECH STOCKS, DON'T BE THE LAST TO HOLD THE BAG

Early replies (first 3)

62311923
60k is the bottom

62311932
>>62311923
>60k is the bottom

>mfw I'm barely clinging to sanity trying to stay hopeful

62311941
>>62311903
I have 50/50 port of ETH and XMR since 2021. 160k in January, 80k now. Is this what I signed up for?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62311947
>>62311903
The bottom will be the SLE, always has been :)

62311953
>>62311932
It won't flip until Trump or one of his sons sends the message. Something like "it's a great time to buy crypto". That's the signal that the bear is over.

62312082
I can tell you exactly what is happening. The Bitcoin crash isn't primarily "retail panic" or "macroeconomics" as some would have you believe... it's a coordinated liquidity harvest by the exact same institutions that have spent the last few years legitimizing Bitcoin through ETFs.

The largest asset managers have spent years now convincing the public that Bitcoin was going to become a mainstream asset. ETF approvals, corporate treasury adoption etc. It all created the impression that Bitcoin was way too big to fail.
The easiest way for large players to acquire Bitcoin cheaply wasn't to buy aggressively themselves but encourage everyone else to buy aggressively. As confidence grows, traders then pile into leveraged long positions.
Once enough traders are underwater, exchanges automatically liquidate positions which forces the prices even lower, triggering even more liquidations. After the ETF investors redeem, after leveraged traders are liquidated and retail capitulates, the same funds will quietly reverse course and accumulate at enormously discounted prices.

62312092
>>62312082
yes, they call it rug pull for a reason, question is how low does it go, other guy said 60k, I think it's 35k

When you sell and butback literally pulses of overpriced electronic pulses, it’s over

he uses private market makers on both operations
this thread is about nothing

62312025

So he can buy high and sell low

item.title: 30% interest on DOT — 4 replies — 7.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
30% interest on DOT

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-03 22:50 UTC

OP

Lets say I buy polkadot at 1 dollar and it pays 3% interest staked

If it goes to 10 dollars and my initial investment is now 10x, does this mean im really getting paid 30% apr on my principal?

Early replies (first 3)

62311023
The other three fucking threads you made didn’t answer your question?

62311850
>>62311017

3% apr at 1 million is actually
30% apr on principal 100k initial investment at 10 dollars y

62311929
>>62311017
Polkashit needs to be $.1 to be fairly valued retard.
Should try buying when bitcoin is at $30k and polkashit nukes another 90% down.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62312020
>>62311017
no, because it doesn't go to 10 dollars, it goes to 10 cents

OP

Are you guys ready?

Near
ICP
TAO
Render

are your safe bets

Early replies (first 3)

62309802
>>62309790
Safe bets are actual real world physical AI indices. Last time I checked Render, they didn't even have a working website lmao.

62309804
>>62309790

62309808
>>62309802
All I’m saying is that anything with AI will explode because crypto people don’t care about tech just the narrative.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62309817
>>62309802
lmao I just checked, they are still selling a queue to a central HPC for proprietary jobs for an outdated rendering engine, with no options. I can have AI code something better in a day.

62309828
>>62309808
Real AI stocks ran since 2023, most did 10x to 100x, while crypto AI lost 95%. No one cares about crypto anymore, stock theme indices don't rug 90% in a week. Keep away from crypto, noobs.

62309843
>>62309828
that’s what people said in 2023 when Bitcoin was 15k. Always buy when people like you say stupid shit like that.

62309888
>>62309843
I can only repeat myself: Hands off crypto. Diversified stock market lets you keep your money. Crypto is gambling. If you want, wait for the next catastrophe Trump creates. I will certainly not buy baseless bullshit crypto tokens in the crypto bear year. If anything, I might buy new low market cap crypto with very well known industry people and connections end of this year, to hold for the next two years, but we are talking about 1% or so of my investment capital, and I will diversify a lot. But certainly not now, no way. Even if one of these shit tokens does a 10x because the crypto scammers pump it, I will not regret this, ever. Gambling is gambling, there's always some lottery winner for the millions of losers, and I am not a cabal insider.

62311741
Woah ICP's at a whole 3 fucking dollars only like 997 dollars left for it to be considered anything decent yah you read that right literally anything less than $1000 a coin is a bust due to all the stress this piece of shit's put everyone through but the chart is filled with baggies so I guarantee it won't ever reach anything close, hell we'd be lucky to see $10 again

62311788
>>62309790

DOT will be a top 5 crypto in 2 years
